headline: GOLD REBOUNDS TO $4,636 AS U.S. Weighs Iran Strikes; Oil Spikes Past $120 in Safe-Haven Surge
snippet: Gold climbed to $4,636.20 on April 30 as reports emerged the U.S. military was briefing President Trump on potential renewed strikes against Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Oil surged past $120 a barrel overnight, triggering fresh safe-haven buying in precious metals while silver followed gold higher.

headline: AMERICAS GOLD SCORES 4TH GALENA DISCOVERY: 1,392 g/t Ag, Six New Idaho Veins 25m From Infrastructure
snippet: Americas Gold and Silver announced its fourth major new discovery at Idaho's Galena Complex, identifying six high-grade silver-copper-antimony veins with a peak intercept of 1,392 g/t Ag, 1.5% Cu, and 1.5% Sb over 1.9m. The 43L-TJ Vein Complex sits just 25 metres from existing mine infrastructure, putting it on a fast track toward production.

headline: ABRASILVER DRILLS 917 g/t Ag OVER 17m At Diablillos; 68.5m at 343 g/t Silver Anchors Phase VI Program
snippet: AbraSilver Resource's Phase VI drilling at Diablillos, Argentina intersected 68.5 metres at 343 g/t silver from 98.5m downhole, including a 17-metre high-grade core at 917 g/t silver at the Oculto East zone. Results confirm strong continuity between the Oculto and JAC zones ahead of a Definitive Feasibility Study targeted for Q2 2026.

headline: RADISSON PUNCHES DEEPEST HOLE EVER AT O'BRIEN: 4.54 g/t Au at 1.9km Depth Extends Abitibi Resource
snippet: Radisson Mining reported 16 new drill holes at its O'Brien Gold Project in Quebec's Abitibi region, with pilot hole OB-26-384 reaching 1.9 kilometres depth — the deepest ever drilled at the project — and returning 4.54 g/t Au over 12.0m. The results extend mineralization below the March 2026 resource estimate as part of an ongoing 140,000-metre step-out program with seven active rigs.

headline: NGEX DISCOVERS NEW COPPER-GOLD ZONE 300m East of Mars at Lunahuasi; 34m at 7.25% CuEq Drilled 180m Away
snippet: NGEx Minerals released Phase 4 results from its Lunahuasi copper-gold-silver project in San Juan, Argentina, hitting 34m at 7.25% CuEq and 48m at 7.72% CuEq 180m apart in the Mars Zone. A new high-grade mineralized structure was discovered 300m east of Mars, cropping just 60m below surface and open in all directions.

headline: USGS: Appalachia Holds 2.3M Tonnes of Undiscovered Lithium — Enough to Replace U.S. Imports for 328 Years
snippet: A landmark USGS assessment estimates 2.3 million metric tonnes of undiscovered, economically recoverable lithium in the Appalachian Mountains — enough to power 130 million EVs. The southern Appalachians (Carolinas) hold 1.43Mt and the northern section (Maine, New Hampshire) holds 900,000t — a potential generational shift in North American battery supply chain security.

headline: MINING M&A HITS $21.6B in Strongest Q1 Since 2023; 121 Deals Up 34% as Critical Mineral Race Accelerates
snippet: Global mining M&A reached $21.6 billion in Q1 2026 — the strongest opening quarter since 2023 — with 121 transactions recorded and aggregate deal value rising 34% year-over-year, per White & Case. Copper, lithium, and critical minerals dominated deal flow as majors raced to lock in supply chain security ahead of anticipated tariff-driven import restrictions.

headline: CENTRAL BANKS SCOOPED 244 TONNES OF GOLD In Q1 at Fastest Pace in Over a Year, WGC Data Shows
snippet: World Gold Council data shows central banks bought 244 tonnes of gold net in Q1 2026 — the fastest accumulation pace in more than a year — as they exploited the 12% pullback from January's record near $5,600/oz. Total gold demand rose 2% year-on-year as bar and coin investment surged even as jewellery demand fell 23%.

headline: COPPER SLIDES FROM $6.12 RECORD AS China Restricts Sulphur Exports; Chile Refining Operations Under Threat
snippet: Copper retreated to $5.89/lb after hitting a record $6.12 on April 22, as stalled U.S.-Iran talks clouded the global manufacturing outlook and China restricted exports of sulphuric acid — a key input for roughly half of Chile's copper refining capacity. Long-term AI infrastructure and grid electrification demand is expected to limit downside, but the Iran conflict is creating near-term supply disruption.

headline: TRACTION URANIUM MAPS AURORA DRILL PUSH In Saskatchewan's Athabasca Basin; Airborne Survey Kicks Off 2026 Season
snippet: Traction Uranium outlined its 2026 exploration roadmap for the Aurora Uranium Project in Saskatchewan's Athabasca Basin, beginning with an airborne radiometric survey to identify near-surface anomalies before advancing to diamond drilling later in the year. The junior sits near NexGen's Rook I and Denison's Wheeler River projects, both now under construction in the world's highest-grade uranium district.

headline: GOLDSEEK: Gold Stocks' Correction Mirrors 1972 Setup — Technical Picture Points Toward $6,000 Target
snippet: GoldSeek analysts argue the current gold stock pullback closely mirrors the 1972 consolidation that preceded gold's biggest historical bull run, with the 1-2 month correction creating a structural entry point before the next leg higher. Federal Reserve policy uncertainty, sustained central bank buying, and persistent Middle East geopolitical risk underpin the $6,000/oz fundamental case.
